name: Entity
summary: An entity is an object shown on a page of a LayOut document.

methods.name: attribute_dictionary
methods.summary: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the entity's attribute dictionary with the given name.
methods.remarks: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the entity's attribute dictionary with the given name. no attribute dictionary

returns.doc: A copy of the entity's attribute dictionary, or nil if there is

methods.name: bounds
methods.summary: The #bounds method returns the 2D rectangular bounds of the Layout::Entity.
methods.remarks: The #bounds method returns the 2D rectangular bounds of the Layout::Entity.

methods.name: delete_attribute
methods.summary: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from an entity.
methods.remarks: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from an entity.

params.name: dictionary_name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

methods.name: document
methods.summary: The #document method returns the Document that the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is not in a Document.
methods.remarks: The #document method returns the Document that the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is not in a Document.

methods.name: drawing_bounds
methods.summary: The #drawing_bounds method returns the 2D rectangular drawing bounds of the Layout::Entity.
methods.remarks: The #drawing_bounds method returns the 2D rectangular drawing bounds of the Layout::Entity.

methods.name: get_attribute
methods.summary: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the entity's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the entity's attribute dictionary. If the third parameter, default_value, is not passed and there is no attribute that matches the given name, it returns nil. If default_value is provided and there is no matching attribute it returns the given value. It does not create an attribute with that name though.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

params.name: default_value
params.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil
params.doc: A default value to return if no attribute is found.

returns.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Layout::Dictionary, nil

methods.name: group
methods.summary: The #group method returns the Group the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is not in a Group.
methods.remarks: The #group method returns the Group the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is not in a Group.

methods.name: layer_instance
methods.summary: Note: Groups are never associated with a LayerInstance.
methods.remarks: Note: Groups are never associated with a LayerInstance. The #layer_instance method returns the LayerInstance that the Layout::Entity is on, or nil if it is not associated with a LayerInstance.

methods.name: locked?
methods.summary: The #locked? method returns whether the Layout::Entity is locked or unlocked.
methods.remarks: The #locked? method returns whether the Layout::Entity is locked or unlocked.

methods.name: move_to_group
methods.summary: The #move_to_group method moves the Layout::Entity into a Group.
methods.remarks: The #move_to_group method moves the Layout::Entity into a Group. If the Layout::Entity is already in a Group, it will be removed from that Group prior to being added to the new one. If this action results in the old Group containing only one Layout::Entity, the old Group will be collapsed and the remaining Layout::Entity will be moved to the old Group's parent.

throws.when: if the Layout::Entity is on a locked Layer
if entity and group are not in the same Document
if entity and group are not on the same Page
if of entity and group one is shared and one is not

methods.name: move_to_layer
methods.summary: The #move_to_layer method moves the Layout::Entity to the given Layer.
methods.remarks: The #move_to_layer method moves the Layout::Entity to the given Layer. If the Layer is non-shared and the Layout::Entity is currently on a shared Layer, an array of Pages must be provided to move the Layout::Entity to. In all other cases, passing in an array of Pages is not necessary. The Layout::Entity must belong to the same Document as the the Layer and the Pages.

throws.when: if layer is shared and a valid array of non-duplicate Pages is not passed in
if entity, layer, and pages are not all in the same Document
if the Layout::Entity is on a locked Layer

methods.name: on_shared_layer?
methods.summary: The #on_shared_layer? method returns whether or not the Layout::Entity is on a shared Layer.
methods.remarks: The #on_shared_layer? method returns whether or not the Layout::Entity is on a shared Layer. This function works for all Layout::Entity types, including Group. Groups do not belong to a specific Layer, but their children are all on either a shared or non-shared Layer.

throws.when: if the Layout::Entity is not in a Document

methods.name: page
methods.summary: The #page method returns the Page that the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is on a shared Layer or not in a Document.
methods.remarks: The #page method returns the Page that the Layout::Entity belongs to, or nil if it is on a shared Layer or not in a Document.

methods.name: set_attribute
methods.summary: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the entity's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the entity's attribute dictionary.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary. @param [String] key An attribute key. @param [String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil] value The value for the attribute.

methods.name: transform!
methods.remarks: Note: Since LayOut 2026.1, passing a non-invertible transformation raises an `ArgumentError`. The #transform! method transforms the Layout::Entity with a given Geom::Transformation2d.

throws.when: if the Layout::Entity is on a locked Layer
if the transformation matrix is not invertible

methods.name: transformation
methods.summary: The #transformation method returns the explicit Geom::Transformation2d.
methods.remarks: The #transformation method returns the explicit Geom::Transformation2d.

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Entitys are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Entitys are equal. This checks whether the Ruby Objects are pointing to the same internal object.

properties.name: locked
properties.summary: The #locked= method sets the Layout::Entity as locked or unlocked.
properties.remarks: The #locked= method sets the Layout::Entity as locked or unlocked. When locked, the Layout::Entity cannot be modified directly.

properties.name: style
properties.summary: The #style method returns the Style of the Layout::Entity.
properties.remarks: The #style method returns the Style of the Layout::Entity. If the Layout::Entity is a Group, nil will be returned, as they do not have a Style.

properties.name: untransformed_bounds
properties.summary: The #untransformed_bounds method returns the untransformed bounds of the Layout::Entity.
properties.remarks: The #untransformed_bounds method returns the untransformed bounds of the Layout::Entity. This is the bounds of the Layout::Entity before its explicit Geom::Transformation2d is applied.
